---
# =====================================================================
#  Press release — EPA compliance workshops paper
#  Conventions: style-guides/press-release/README.md
#  Template:    formats/press-release/press-release-template.qmd
#  Render:  quarto render epa-compliance-workshops_press-release.qmd
#  Design system: ../../../formats/press-release/_style.tex
# =====================================================================
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - top=0.6in
      - bottom=0.55in
      - left=0.75in
      - right=0.75in
    include-in-header:
      - file: ../../../formats/press-release/_style.tex
    keep-tex: true
---

```{=latex}
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MASTHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\briefheader{\includegraphics[height=0.45in]{../../../formats/logos/eil-logo-maroon.png}}{Press Release}

% Gap below the masthead rule to the headline (~7pt, matching the research
% highlight's headsep; the briefheader macro adds -3pt, so 10pt nets ~7pt)
\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% HEADLINE + SUBHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
{\hyphenpenalty=10000\exhyphenpenalty=10000
\brieftitle{Free EPA Compliance Workshops Did Not Reduce Pollution Violations, Study Finds}}
\vspace{1pt}
\noindent{\color{cite}\rmfamily\itshape\fontsize{11pt}{14pt}\selectfont A program the EPA is now expanding to more states showed no measurable effect on the facilities it was meant to help.\par}
\vspace{2pt}
\noindent\textcolor{warmrule}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.7pt}}

\vspace{8pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% RELEASE LINE + DATELINE (opens the body, AEI-style) + LEAD
% Embargo form: replace with EMBARGOED UNTIL [Month Day, Year, Time TZ]
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\bfseries\color{accentred}\fontsize{8.25pt}{10pt}\selectfont\textls[50]{FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE}}\hspace{0.6em}{\color{muted}\fontsize{9pt}{12pt}\selectfont CHARLOTTESVILLE, VA · [Month Day, Year]}\par
\vspace{5pt}
\noindent Offering polluters free help to follow environmental rules did not make them any more likely to follow them, according to a new study of hundreds of wastewater treatment facilities in Ohio. The research, published in the \textit{Journal of Policy Analysis and Management}, found that compliance workshops promoted by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) had no detectable effect on violations.

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BODY
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent The study examined the EPA's alternative to inspections and fines: ``compliance assistance'' --- free workshops, guidance, and technical support. The approach is built on the premise that many facilities break the rules out of confusion rather than deliberate corner-cutting. Using detailed Ohio EPA records, authors Paul J. Ferraro and Jay P. Shimshack compared facilities trained earlier with comparable facilities that had also volunteered but were not yet trained. Measured against that benchmark, violations at trained facilities did not fall.

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent The finding is notable because the program had appeared to work: the EPA's own before-and-after figures showed violations dropping by more than 50 percent after the workshops. But the same decline was occurring at facilities that had never been trained --- a broad downward trend easy to mistake for the workshops' effect. Encouraged by that apparent success, the EPA is now expanding the program to other states. Small benefits cannot be ruled out, the authors note, but this study suggests that compliance assistance is not an effective strategy for reducing violations.

\vspace{15pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MEDIA CONTACT  (AEI-style: above the ###, body size, bold lead-in)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\fontsize{10pt}{13.5pt}\selectfont
{\bfseries For questions, interviews, or media inquiries,} contact [Full Name] at [email] or [phone].\par}

\vspace{11pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% END MARKER
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{faint}\bfseries \#\#\#}\end{center}

\vspace{11pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BOILERPLATE  (centered, italic, org name as link)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{muted}\itshape\fontsize{9.5pt}{14pt}\selectfont
The \href{https://www.environmental-inequality-lab.org/}{\color{cite}\underline{Environmental Inequality Lab}} is a nonpartisan research group that applies a rigorous, data-driven approach to understand how our environment shapes economic opportunity and well-being.\par}
\end{center}

\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% PAPER LINK
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{faint}\fontsize{8pt}{11pt}\selectfont
Full paper: \href{https://doi.org/10.1002/pam.70056}{\color{faint}\underline{doi.org/10.1002/pam.70056}} \quad·\quad
Research highlight: \href{[URL]}{\color{faint}\underline{[link]}}\par}
\end{center}
```
